author: andy-stack
description: Multi-AI assistant. Read, search, create, and edit notes with multiple AI providers. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Andy-Stack
manifest.description: Multi-AI assistant. Read, search, create, and edit notes with multiple AI providers.
manifest.name: Vaultkeeper AI

name: Vaultkeeper AI

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release brings file history to the plugin for agent actions (create, modify and delete).

### Notable features
- Any time the agent creates, updates or deletes a note the changes are tracked
- The chat window displays a list of all affected files
- You can check what changes were made in past turns using the diff viewer
- You can restore notes to the before/after state for any message it is listen in
- Binary files (images, PDF etc) are also supported (only in terms of deletion as the plugin doesn't support creating/editing these)
- Fixed a few minor bugs and updated OpenAI to the latest round of models.
releases.name: 1.4.9 - File History

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: A hotfix release to address and issue that prevented settings from loading correctly.
releases.name: 1.4.8 - Settings Hotfix

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This is a larger release that introduces local models, enhancements to planning mode and various other goodies.

### Notable Changes
- Support for connecting to locally hosted models is now live
- Planning mode now includes a plan refinement/approval stage
- A new toggle to allow the agent to make changes without approval (no diff check)
- Updated UI for agent thoughts and an Obsidian graph graph like animation for the empty chat area
- Various UI tweaks to keep the mobile experience user friendly
- Remote model updates
releases.name: 1.4.7 - Local Model Support

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This release just addresses code quality warnings identified by Obsidian's automated plugin scanner.
releases.name: 1.4.6 - Code Quality

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This release removes a dependency that was being used for .docx parsing that was bringing in transitive dependencies that were causing issues with Obsidian's plugin scanner. Parsing of .docx is now handed internally like other office formats.
releases.name: 1.4.5 - Plugin Dependency (docx parsing)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release fixes some bugs, refines some dependencies and prepares for local model support.

### Notable Changes
- Fixed bug where Gemini models were displayed incorrectly
- Replace heavy PDF and document dependencies for obsidian bundled and lighter alternatives
- Refactor of AI classes to prepare for OpenAPI Chat Completions supported local models
releases.name: 1.4.4 - Maintenance and Bug Fixes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This release compresses the plugin banner to significantly reduce the plugin size. Previously this was an uncompressed PNG.
releases.name: 1.4.3 - Compress plugin banner

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release addresses an issue on mobile where a missing referenced asset (plugin banner) caused the plugin to fail to load.

### Notable Changes
- All assets are now bundled inline instead of as separate assets to better support Obsidian on mobile
releases.name: 1.4.2 - Mobile bug fix

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Added new CONTRIBUTING.md file. Releasing to trigger the automated Obsidian plugin review.
releases.name: 1.4.1 - Contributing guide

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Removed an unused .wasm reference flagged by Obsidian's plugin health scan. The reference came from a PDF dependency and was never functional — no WASM binary was ever bundled.
releases.name: 1.4.0 - Plugin Review

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release introduces a custom plugin icon and banner, improves how the AI handles file attachments, and hopefully addresses the final Obsidian plugin scan warnings.

### Notable Changes
- Replaced the generic sparkles icon with a custom VaultKeeper icon throughout the app (sidebar, ribbon, and quick actions menu)
- Added the new plugin banner to the help modal welcome section and updated the README to use it
- Updated prompts to improve how the AI recognizes/handles binary files (PDFs, images, and documents)
releases.name: 1.3.9 - Custom Icon, Banner and Reliability

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Small release that addresses the remaining warnings detected by Obsidian's Community Plugin scanner.
releases.name: 1.3.8 - Plugin Review

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release adds new AI-powered Quick Actions for working with notes, refreshes the available AI models, and improves the in-app help menu.

### Notable Changes
- Added new quick actions, quick actions now include: Proofread, Beautify, Apply template, Apply links, Apply tags, Suggest tags and Generate frontmatter 
- Updated the list of available AI models for all providers to reflect the latest recommendations
- Reorganised the help modal into clearer, more granular topics and added a detailed Quick Actions section covering every action 
- Small improvements to the agent system prompts around Obsidian specific markdown
releases.name: 1.3.7 - New Quick Actions and Model Updates

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release focuses on minor bug fixes and simplifying internal code.

### Notable Changes
- Fixed a bug that prevented the web search toggle and chat mode toggle from changing (visual only)
- Simplified how chat scrolling works
- Simplified styles related to diff checks to hopefully respect themes a bit better
- Simplified chat markdown rendering by moving from Unified to Obsidian's MarkdownRenderer
- Added a new 'scroll to bottom' button (appears when scrolled away from the bottom)
releases.name: 1.3.6 - Bug Fixes and Refinement

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This release addresses some of the automated comments from the plugin review scan and updated dependencies.
releases.name: 1.3.5 - Obsidian Review Comments

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release mostly focuses on under the hood improvements and maintenance with a slight change to how chat mode is handled.

### Notable Changes
- Chat mode is now remembered between conversations, restarts and theoretically through Obsidian Sync
- A rare agent hallucination that allowed edits to made made when in read only mode has been patched
releases.name: 1.3.4 - Chat Mode Refinements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release fixes a few annoyances and adds the latest GPT model.

### Notable Changes
- Strengthened system prompt messaging regarding web search when enabled
- Adjust default Obsidian elements when chat input is focused on mobile (can be toggled off in settings)
- Replace GPT 5.4 with GPT 5.5
releases.name: 1.3.3 - Stability & Polish

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release brings quick actions to mobile.

### Notable Changes
- Quick actions can now be accessed through right click context menu and the editor tool bar
- Quick actions can be toggled in the plugin settings (context menu and editor tool bar)
releases.name: v1.3.2 - Quick Actions on Mobile

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description: Small release to fix a bug introduced by the new chat selection that prevented user instructions from being selected.
releases.name: v1.3.1 - Bug fix

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release introduces a redesigned chat mode system, a new quick actions feature, and several UI and mobile improvements.

This release introduces 'Quick Actions' and also reworks some of the UI/UX on mobile and desktop.

### Notable Changes
- The right click context menu on desktop now has quick actions for 'Beautify' and 'Apply template'
- The settings menu has been extended so an AI model can be set for use with quick actions 
- Switching chat modes is now done through a unified selector
- A new 'Add attachments' button has been added to allow attaching files from the file explorer on all platforms
- Cleanup of dependency versions and linting
releases.name: v1.3.0 - Quick Actions and UI

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release updates the Open AI models (removing old ones) and adds a new Gemini model.

### Notable Changes
- Replace GPT 5, 5.1 and 5.2 with the new GPT 5.4 range of models
- Add Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite Preview
releases.name: v1.2.9 - AI Model Changes

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release adds persistent AI memory, web search toggle and Obsidian web viewer capabilities.

### Notable Changes
- A new Memories file in the plugin folder allows the AI to keep persistent memories. Memories can be edited, enabled or disabled through settings at any time
-  A new toggle for web search has been added and web search capabilities has been added to Mistral (this was previously missing)
- The AI can now view content in the Obsidian web viewer core plugin. This can also be enabled or disabled in the plugin settings at any time.
- A few new tools for the agent to allow better folder management
releases.name: v1.2.8 - Memories, Web Tools & Vault Folder Management

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release fixes document parsing on Obsidian mobile and improves the chat input layout.

### Notable Changes

- Fix document parsing (docx, pptx, etc.) on Obsidian mobile by switching to the officeparser browser bundle
- The chat input moves above the input buttons on desktop for longer messages to give more breathing room when typing (this is already the default layout on mobile)
releases.name: v1.2.7 - Mobile Compatibility & UI Refinements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release adds support for various document formats, refines some aspects of the chat UI and some under the hood improvements.

### Notable Changes
- Add support for docx, pptx, xlsx, odt, odp, ods and rtf file types
- Adjust the UI for large user messages so they don't dominate the chat area
releases.name: v1.2.6 - Document Support & UI Improvements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release fixes some small bugs and adds Obsidian Bases knowledge to the system prompts.

### Notable Changes
- Add Obsidian Bases documentation to relevant system prompts (many agents have knowledge cutoff before Bases were released)
- Fixed bug where example instructions were asking for write permission during plugin first load
- Fix some minor UI color inconsistencies
releases.name: v1.2.5 - Bases & Bugs

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release adds Mistral as a new provider.

### Notable Changes
- Add new Mistral models:
    - Mistral Large (latest)
    - Mistral Medium (latest)
    - Mistral Small (latest)
 - Removed 'gemini-3-pro-preview' as it will be retired soon (use gemini-3.1-pro-preview)
releases.name: v1.2.4 - Mistral AI

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release focuses on further planning mode refinements, adding new provider models and a minor tool improvement.

### Notable Changes
- Changed planning mode so that adjustments in the plan are facilitated by the orchestration agent rather than requiring a full re-plan
- Orchestration agent now uses the same model as the planning agent
- The execution agent now preserves context between steps (allowing for read file steps without the need for explicit context passing)
- The latest models for Claude and Gemini have been added
- The GPT4 range of models and Sonnet 3.7 have been retired
- The patch file tool now allows the agent to make multiple edits to a note at once
releases.name: v1.2.3 - Planning Mode Refinements & New Models

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release focuses on stability, performance and bug fixes.

### Notable Changes
- Fixed a bug where the chat would auto scroll even if the user had scrolled up
- Automatically resize images before API requests (avoid image size limits)
- Fix unsupported attachment types for providers that only accept plain text mime type
- Restructure conversation attachments to store binary data separately
- Added UI indications of when long running tool calls are taking place (not currently supported by Gemini)
- Made improvements to the system prompts of planning related agents
- Fix plan not auto scrolling for long running plans
releases.name: v1.2.2 - Stability and Performance

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description: A minor release to add Claude Opus 4.6 to the list of available models. Also bumped some dependency versions.
releases.name: v1.2.1 - Claude Opus 4.6

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description: A minor release to fix a small bug that caused the re-plan request from the orchestration agent to get stuck in a loop.
releases.name: v1.2.0 - Hotfix and package updates

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release focuses on tweaks to planning mode execution and mobile UI refinements. The execution agent now has stronger boundaries to prevent scope creep, and the planning agent has been given a more robust multi-tier search strategy.

### Notable Changes
- Stricter system prompts for planning mode related agents
- Added new flag to all providers around enforcing tool calling (useful for planning mode agents)
- Mobile UI improvements: fixed modal margins, conversation history grid sizing, and diff view height calculations on screens ≤600px
releases.name: v1.1.9 - Agent Refinements & Mobile UI

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release again focuses on refinements to planning mode. The multi agent system has been completely refactored from the ground up. A few minor bug fixes have also gone in, including UI fixes after the Obsidian mobile UI update.

#### Notable Changes
- Planning mode has gone from '*Main*, *Planning*' agents to '*Main*, *Orchestration*, *Planning*, *Execution*' agents
- Planning mode now marks progress reliably
- Planning mode can recover from unexpected changes to the plan
- Minor UI updates to ensure usability on mobile after the new Obsidian mobile UI changes
releases.name: v1.1.8 - Planning Mode Refactor & UI

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release focuses on refinements to planning mode to drastically improve reliability across providers.

### Notable Changes
- Add the ability for the executing agent to ask for user input mid plan execution
- Adjust planning agent prompt to avoid 'over-planning' (typically an issue for GPT 5+)
- Drastically improve the reliability of agents following given plans (marking steps as complete)
- Fixed a few bugs to do with re-planning and plan cancellation
- Minor bug & UI fixes
releases.name: v1.1.7 - Planning Mode Refinements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release introduces a new two-agent planning workflow for complex tasks, along with improved rate limit handling across providers.

### Notable Changes
- New Planning Mode: Enable a two-agent workflow where a planning agent creates step-by-step execution plans before the main agent takes action. Includes interactive questions to clarify requirements, a dedicated planning model setting, and plan cancellation support.
- Rate Limit Countdown UI: Visual countdown timer when rate limits are hit, with provider-specific retry delay parsing for Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini.
- Markdown Support in Planning Questions: AI questions during planning render with proper markdown formatting (emphasis, lists, code snippets).
- Improved Conversation Naming: Added prompt injection protection and better validation for auto-generated conversation titles.

### Fixes & Improvements
- Fix conversation deletion bug that prevented saves after delete
- Updated dependencies (diff2html, zod, TypeScript ESLint, rollup)
releases.name: v1.1.6 - Planning Mode

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release updates the UI for chat attachments and fixes some minor issues.

### Notable Changes
- Update attachments UI to be clearer (more obvious that it is an attachment to the message)
- Fix issue where dragging and dropping a file could cause the file to appear as two duplicate attachments
- Improve input responsiveness when adding attachments (animation)
releases.name: v1.1.5 - UI Update & Fixes

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release attempts to fix files API upload failures for Gemini specifically on mobile (other providers are fine)

- No notable changes other than fixes
releases.name: v1.1.4 - Fix Mobile Files API (Gemini)

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release attempts to fix the files API upload issue experienced on mobile.

### Notable changes
- Replace Node.js Buffer API usage for standard JS types
- Minor changes to request headers and logic that may have been causing issues
releases.name: v1.1.3 - Fix Mobile Files API

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release adds new conversation attachments support as well as some minor fixes / improvements.

### Notable Changes
- Support for dragging and dropping files from within Obsidian or the external file system directly to the chat
- Support for pasting files directly into the chat (files are added as attachments to the chat)
- New UI making it clear what files are being included in a message
- New UI to show which files were previously included in a message
- AI is now given context (path) for the active file (as long as the active file is not listed in the exclusions)
releases.name: v1.1.2 - Attachment Support

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release extends the supported vault content that the agent can interact with, as well as some other minor fixes and improvements.

### Notable Changes
- Added support for the Files API for Claude, Gemini and OpenAI
- Files API allows the agent to read binary files such as PDF's and Images
- Updated settings and help to include mentions of managing files uploaded to the files API
- Removed the token counter - this wasn't providing much value and isn't worth the maintenance overhead given more than just text content is now supported
- Minor UI improvements
releases.name: v1.1.1 - PDF Search & Binary File Support (PDF, Image, etc)

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
Minor release to fix a few minor display issues on mobile.

### Notable Changes
- Fix conversation name and plugin close button positions on mobile
- Fix plugin window not focusing after dismissing diff viewer
releases.name: v1.1.0 - Mobile UX Refinements / Fixes

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release focuses on bug fixes and refinements.

### Notable Changes
- Fix an issue where past function calls would confuse the LLM when switching provider mid conversation
- Significantly reduce the token size of the base system prompt with no noticeable performance loss (actually a slight improvement)
- Improve input focusing on mobile to prevent the keyboard from opening at undesirable times
- Improve chat area scrolling position and timing when streaming messages
releases.name: v1.0.9 - Bug Fixes & Mobile UX Refinements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release focuses on improving cross-provider AI compatibility, enabling seamless switching between Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini while maintaining conversation context. It includes major API migrations, UX refinements, and code quality improvements.

### Notable Changes
- OpenAI Responses API Migration: Migrated from Chat Completions to Responses API with proper input/output item handling
- Gemini 3 Thought Signature Support: Implemented thought signature tracking for Gemini function calls to meet Gemini 3 requirements
- Cross-Provider Conversation Compatibility: Added legacy text format fallback for function calls to allow seamless provider switching without losing conversation context
- Improved Chat Layout: Added ResizeObserver for dynamic content sizing and better indicator height calculations
- Enhanced Mobile UX: Improved keyboard focus handling in diff view for mobile devices
- Search Query Fix: Fixed search query extraction to use cursor position instead of full text
releases.name: v1.0.8 - Cross-Provider Compatibility & Enhanced AI Integration

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release introduces an interactive diff viewer that allows you to review and approve AI-proposed changes before they're applied to your vault, along with improved file editing capabilities and mobile support.

### Notable Changes
- Interactive diff viewer with approve/reject workflow for AI file changes
- Mobile support for diff review with touch-friendly accept/reject buttons
- New file patching system for making targeted changes without replacing entire files
- Centralized abort controller with visual cancellation feedback
- Add support for Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini 3 Pro Preview, and GPT-5.1
- Improved error handling with structured API errors and automatic retry logic
- Performance tests for vault cache service
- Enhanced chat service lifecycle and diff handling reliability
- Dependency updates
releases.name: v1.0.7 - Interactive Diff Viewer & Enhanced File Editing

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
This release adds web search capabilities to Claude and the OpenAI models (Gemini web search support was already included).

#### Notable Changes

- Web search for Claude and OpenAI
- Remove 'gpt-4.1-nano' support since it doesn't support web search
- Improve UX on mobile when selecting auto completions and agent instruction templates
- Migrate OpenAI API to newer Responses API
- Document 503 errors in troubleshooting section
releases.name: v1.0.6 - AI Web Search & other Improvements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
#### Performance Improvements

- Significantly improved vault search times

#### Bug Fixes

- LLM provided regex searches not being parsed
- Autocompletes not working on mobile (attempted fix)
- Other minor improvements
releases.name: v1.0.5 - Bug fixes and performance improvements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
#### This release focuses on bug fixes and improvements

- Fix issue with conversation history loading (regression from ESLint fixes)
- Fix issue with Claude function call history not having valid ID's when swapping from another provider
- Improve agent system prompt to better follow the latest guidance online
- Minor object type improvements
releases.name: v1.0.4 - Bug fixes and improvements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
##### Improve markdown processor type safety and plugin order

- Replace any types with explicit unified processor types (MdastRoot, HastRoot)
- Move wikiLinkPlugin before remarkRehype to fix processing order
releases.name: v1.0.3 - Minor release to fix Unified ESLint error

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
##### Minor release that addresses the remaining ESLint issues.

- Match ESLint config to Obsidian config
- Add comments to ESLint exclusions
- Minor code changes to address ESLint issues
releases.name: v1.0.2 - Address ESLint Errors

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
#### This release focuses on improving TypeScript type safety and code quality across the codebase.

#### Key Improvements:
- Added ESLint to the project with Obsidian-recommended configuration
- Added Zod validation schemas for all AI function arguments
- Replaced custom type definitions with official SDK types from @anthropic-ai/sdk, openai, and @google/genai
- Added explicit type assertions and proper TypeScript interfaces for streaming events and function parameters
- Added intentional ESLint disable comments where necessary
releases.name: v1.0.1 - Type Safety and Code Quality Improvements

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
Brings Claude, Gemini, and OpenAI directly into your Obsidian vault with intelligent note management.

## Key Features

- **Multi-Provider Support**: Switch between Claude, Gemini, and OpenAI models
- **Dual Operating Modes**: Read-only mode for safe analysis, Agent mode for note editing
- **Smart References**: Autocomplete for tags (`#`), files (`@`), and folders (`/`)
- **Custom Instructions**: Personalize AI behavior for different workflows
- **Privacy-First**: API keys stored locally, file exclusions with glob patterns
- **Conversation History**: Persistent chats with auto-generated titles
- **Mobile Compatible**: Full functionality when using the Obsidian mobile app

## Notes

- API usage billed by respective providers
- All data stored locally in your vault
- No third-party data sharing
releases.name: Initial Release: Multi-Provider AI Agent for Obsidian

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
- Refactor chat area scrolling to fix 'jitter' and occasional positioning issues
- Added extensive unit and integration tests
- New streaming indicator (more professional IMO)
- Added new feature allowing "@, /, #" to provide content to the AI for Files, Folders and Tags respectively.
- Other minor bug fixes
releases.name: BETA 0.0.4

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description:
- Breaking change: Refactor how chats are stored, now handle cases where AI returns function call and response in same payload. Chats from previous versions will not work.
- New models are available from Claude, OpenAI and Gemini
- Conversations are now ordered by most recently used instead of most recently created
- Bump TypeScript and package versions
releases.name: BETA 0.0.3

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description: - Minor bug fixes
releases.name: BETA 0.0.2

releases.author: Andy-Stack
releases.description: Initial BETA release.
releases.name: BETA Release
